result.JobDescription:
Overview:
Seeking a skilled and experienced interventional radiology technologist to join our team. The ideal candidate will have a minimum of 2 years of experience and possess essential certifications and skills. This position offers normal business hours with a rotating day off and call requirements as per contract.

Schedule:
• Hours: 7:30am - 6:00pm, Monday - Friday with a rotating day off.
• Call Requirement: One primary weekday and one backup weekday/month along with one weekend every 6 weeks that would alternate between primary or backup.

Requirements:
• Experience: Minimum 2 years in interventional radiology.
• Certifications: ACLS, BLS, ARRT.
• Skills: Proficient in using Siemens equipment.
• Preferred: Experience with EPIC electronic medical records system.

Key Responsibilities:
• Perform interventional radiology procedures.
• Operate Siemens imaging equipment effectively.
• Ensure patient safety and comfort throughout procedures.
• Maintain accurate patient records in EPIC (preferred).

This is a variable shift position, where traveler will work days/evenings, 8s, 10s, or 12s. Call required M-F 2100-630. St and Sun 630-1900 and 1900-630 with holiday/weekend coverage may be required based on unit needs. Please follow the below to pipeline accurately.

Overview: Join our team in a Level 1 Trauma 18-bed Operating Room. We perform a variety of surgeries, including General, Robotic, Laparoscopic, Trauma, Orthopedic, Neurosurgery, Spinal, Ophthalmology, ENT, Plastics, Burns, Urology, GYN, and Transplant.

Responsibilities:
•	Provide care during surgical procedures.
•	Circulate, focusing on Orthopedic, Neurosurgery, Spine, and General Surgery cases.
•	Work closely with the surgical team.
•	Maintain patient records in EPIC.

Requirements:
•	2 years of OR RN experience.
•	Experience in Level 1 trauma centers.
•	EPIC and BCLS certification.

Preferred Experience: ENT, GU, Plastics, Burns

The PT will work M-F in the Genesis Outpatient Pediatrics Rehab Center, working 36 hours/week, five 8-hr days (four 10's is NOT a possibility). At least two years of current experience with pediatrics rehab is required as this PT will cover an existing caseload of 50 patients. BLS required. Ohio license and registry required (cannot be in process). Candidates with multiple days of RTO requests will be low priority candidates.

result.JobTitle: Perm - Physician - Hospitalist - Pediatric Reno NV
result.JobDescription:
Pediatrix Medical Group is seeking a full-time Board Certified/Board Eligible Pediatric Hospitalist to join its well-established and collaborative pediatric care team at Renown Regional Medical Center in Reno, Nevada, offering a unique opportunity to practice high-quality inpatient pediatric medicine in a Level II Trauma Center with strong subspecialty support, academic affiliation, and access to world-class outdoor living. 

Job Description 
  • Provide pediatric inpatient ward coverage in a 30-bed unit with an average daily census of 16–18 patients  
  • Perform newborn nursery examinations  
  • Conduct Emergency Department pediatric consultations  
  • Collaborate with a multidisciplinary pediatric care team, including subspecialists and intensivists  
  • Work closely with a 16-bed PICU staffed by dedicated pediatric intensivists  
  • Participate in teaching and education of pediatric residents and medical students  
  • Engage in an academic environment through affiliation with the University of Nevada, Reno School of Medicine  
  • Deliver care in a Level II Trauma Center serving northern Nevada and northeastern California  
  • Ensure high-quality, patient-centered pediatric care aligned with Pediatrix standards  
  • This is a permanent, full-time position that requires residency within the immediate local area.  Relocation may be required.
  • Not accepting Locums candidates
  • Not accepting visa candidates
Compensation and Benefits:
  • Competitive salary. base salary range is $190K - $230K (based on years of experience and training; discussed during interview)  
  • Comprehensive benefits package including:  
  • Medical, Dental, Vision insurance  
  • Life and Disability coverage  
  • Healthcare FSA & Dependent Care FSA  
  • Health Savings Account (HSA) options  
  • 401(k) retirement plan  
  • Employee Stock Purchase Program  
  • Access to voluntary benefits via payroll deduction  
  • Supportive employer culture focused on employee well-being and work-life balance  
  • Opportunity to live in a no state income tax location with exceptional lifestyle benefits  

Requirement description : ALL OF THE FOLLOWING SHOULD BE NOTED IN ADDITIONAL SUBMISSION DETAILS. REQUIRED PRIOR TO CLIENT PRESENTATION UNLESS OTHERWISE NOTED

  • Board Certified or Board Eligible in Pediatrics – [REQUIRED] 
  • Nevada Medical License (or willingness to obtain) – [REQUIRED] 
  • Nevada Medical License or eligibility to obtain – [REQUIRED]  
  • Most recent NPDB Self Query Report (within 30 days active) - [REQUIRED] * no exemption, required at presentation
  • Clean malpractice and medical license history – [HIGHLY PREFERRED] 
  • Does provider already live in the area or they willing to relocate? (note in "Additional Submission Details" field) - [REQUIRED] 
  • Does the provider require visa support - J1 or H1B? (note in "Additional Submission Details" field) - [REQUIRED] 
  • DEA (must be obtained prior to start) - (note in "Additional Submission Details" field) [REQUIRED]  * no exemptions
  • CSR/CDS (must be obtained prior to start) - (note in "Additional Submission Details" field) [REQUIRED]  * no exemptions
Pediatrix Medical Group conducts provider clearance on a facility-by-facility basis, not Pediatrix-wide.

By submitting your applicant to this need you are acknowledging that you have checked and have no inclination that your candidate has applied directly to this position. Any candidate that has applied directly is not eligible to be onboarded through an agency.
CERTIFICATION REQUIREMENTS :
  • Board Certified
  • Board Eligible

STATE LICENSE REQUIREMENTS :
  • Nevada

ADDITIONAL LICENSE REQUIREMENTS : Does the provider require visa support - J1 or H1B?; Does provider already live in the area or they willing to relocate?
Weekend Requirements : Likely required as part of hospitalist schedule (hours not specified)
On Call Requirements : Not specified

result.JobTitle: Perm - Physician - Hospitalist - Pediatric Reno NV
result.JobDescription:
Pediatrix Medical Group is seeking a full-time Newborn Pediatric Hospitalist (MD/DO) to join its well-established pediatric hospitalist team at Renown Regional Medical Center in Reno, Nevada, offering an exciting opportunity to practice high-quality newborn hospital medicine in a collaborative, well-supported environment, with academic affiliation and comprehensive subspecialty support.

Job Description:
The provider will be responsible for:
  • Performing newborn nursery examinations
  • Performing circumcisions
  • Participating in pediatric resident and medical student education
  • Working alongside Pediatrix-affiliated pediatric intensivists, hospitalists, and neonatologists
  • Collaborating with dedicated newborn nursing staff trained in transitional, newborn, and couplet care
  • Leveraging Certified Lactation Specialist support available 365 days/year
  • Practicing in a 58-bed Level 3 NICU with 24/7 in-house neonatology coverage
  • Utilizing comprehensive pediatric subspecialty support, including pediatric surgery
  • No delivery attendance required
  • This is a permanent, full-time position that requires residency within the immediate local area.  Relocation may be required.
  • Not accepting Locums candidates
  • Not accepting visa candidates

Compensation and Benefits:
  • Competitive salary. base salary range is $190K - $230K (based on years of experience and training; discussed during interview)  
  • Sign-on bonuses and relocation assistance may be offered
  • Benefits include:
  • Medical, dental, and vision coverage
  • Life and disability insurance
  • Healthcare FSA, Dependent Care FSA, and HSA options
  • 401(k) retirement plan and Employee Stock Purchase Program
  • Voluntary plans available via payroll deductions
  • Comprehensive support for health, well-being, and work-life balance
  • Collaborative environment emphasizing patient-centered care and clinical excellence

Requirement description : ALL OF THE FOLLOWING SHOULD BE NOTED IN ADDITIONAL SUBMISSION DETAILS. REQUIRED PRIOR TO CLIENT PRESENTATION UNLESS OTHERWISE NOTED

  • Board Certified or Board Eligible in Pediatrics – [REQUIRED] 
  • Nevada Medical License (or willingness to obtain) – [REQUIRED] 
  • Nevada Medical License or eligibility to obtain – [REQUIRED]  
  • Most recent NPDB Self Query Report (within 30 days active) - [REQUIRED] * no exemption, required at presentation
  • Clean malpractice and medical license history – [HIGHLY PREFERRED] 
  • Does provider already live in the area or they willing to relocate? (note in "Additional Submission Details" field) - [REQUIRED] 
  • Does the provider require visa support - J1 or H1B? (note in "Additional Submission Details" field) - [REQUIRED] 
  • DEA (must be obtained prior to start) - (note in "Additional Submission Details" field) [REQUIRED]  * no exemptions
  • CSR/CDS (must be obtained prior to start) - (note in "Additional Submission Details" field) [REQUIRED]  * no exemptions
Pediatrix Medical Group conducts provider clearance on a facility-by-facility basis, not Pediatrix-wide.

  • Board Eligible

On Call Requirements : Overnight emergent coverage in the newborn nursery handled by neonatologists/NICU team (no on-call required for the provider)

result.JobTitle: SMH Level 4 RN OR RNFA - Robotics
result.JobDescription:
Main OR – Operating Room RFNA for Davinci Robotics – Registered Nurse

 Davinci Experience Required:

36 or 48hr/wk

Monday-Friday days shifts

On callresponsibilities per unit need (until 11pm only)

No weekends, holidays per unit need




Job Duties:
RNFA is a highly trained RN who must have both a CNOR certification and have completed an RNFA program which is offered through a college or university. Collaborates with the surgeon and the healthcare team in performing a safe operation with optimal outcomes for the patient. Practices perioperative nursing and must have acquired the necessary knowledge, skill & judgment specific to clinical practice in the operating room and in the RNFA role. Practices in collaboration with and at the direction of the surgeon in the operative phase of the perioperative experience. Performs duties of OR staff RN when not assigned in RNFA role-NYS license as a Registered Professional Nurse. Completion of an accredited RN First Assistant course. Robotic surgery experience preferred. Proficient in perioperative nursing practice-preferred scrubbing and circulating experience. No weekends, but will have evening call and rotating holiday requirements. CNOR required. Combined Circ/Scrub and RNFA role.

Job Requirements:
- RN License (NY License ONLY)
- Davinci Experience Required:

  • Robotic Urology
  • Robotic GYN
  • Robotic Colorectal
  • Robotic Surgical Oncology (whipples, gastrectomies, hernias, etc)
  • Robotic Thoracic
  • Robotic general surgery (gall bladders, appendectomies)


-  CNOR Required
- Two (2) years Experience OR experience in a level 1 trauma preferred

Certification(s):
- BLS (American Heart Association ONLY)
- ACLS preferred (American Heart Association ONLY)
-CNOR Certification

Required/Desired Skills


SkillRequired /DesiredAmountof Experience
RN License (NY License ONLY)Required0
BLS Certification (American Heart Association ONLY)Required0
Two (2) Years ExperienceRequired2Years
able to scrub and circulate preferredRequired0
Davinci RequiredRequired0
ACLS Certification (American Heart Association ONLY)Nice to have0
CNOR Certification Required0

Sunday - Thursday, 3:30pm - Midnight; 5x8hrs Evenings, on call/callback and holidays per unit needs

***Please note, must be able to report within 30 minutes to on call shifts***

ON CALL: 2-3 call shifts per month

X-Ray Technologist Responsibilities:

·        Verifying patients' identity and reviewing physicians’ orders before beginning x-ray procedures.

·        Using x-ray equipment to take radiographic images of patients' bones, tissues, and organs as per physicians’ written orders

·        Explaining x-ray procedures to patients and answering their questions.

·        Positioning patients accordingly, which may include lifting and moving patients with limited mobility.

·        Handles all radiologic equipment safely and properly keeping dose to patient as low as possible.

·        Observe each patient’s status and interprets the appropriate information needed to meet his or her age-specific needs.

·        Produces a diagnostic, high quality image for interpretation by the Radiologist.

·        Promptly notifying management of damaged or malfunctioning x-ray equipment.

·        Utilizes electronic patient information systems to verify patient name, date, appropriate exam, and any special exam specific comments from the provider. Understands and practices the Dual patient identifier procedure.

·        Maintains accurate patient record keeping by properly identifying images and assuring images are sent to the PACS system correctly.

X-Ray Technologist Requirements:

·        Completion of formal training in an approved School of Radiologic Technology and maintains Certification through the American Registry of Radiologic Technologists (ARRT).

·        NYS license (or able to obtain license prior to starting)

·        2 years of X-ray Technologist experience

·        Level 1 trauma center experience

·        Ability to move or lift patients.

·        Ability to stand for extended periods.

·        Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills.

·        Effective written and verbal communication skills.

·        A patient and compassionate disposition.

·        Team player who can maintain a cohesive working environment.



Ability to report within 30 minutes to on call shift. 

REQUIREMENTS: 

BLS

Experience using c-arm/o-arm in the past 8 months

****Must have recent OR experience ****


Required/Desired Skills


SkillRequired /DesiredAmountof Experience
Technician License (currently ANY Tech License is accepted in the state of NY)Required0
Two (2) years experienceRequired0
BLS (American Heart Association ONLY)Required0
Experience using c-arm/o-armRequired8Months
trauma 1 center experienceRequired0

5x8hr Days, on call/callback/holiday per unit needs; 

*** Will consider 6 month contractors ***


On Call: 5-7 call shifts per 7 week time block (2-4 of these days will be weekend call)

Interventional Radiology

PRIMARY DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES:

Demonstrates competence in selecting exposure factors, positioning and consistently produces radiologic images of diagnostic quality. Works cooperatively with all members of the department to resolve problems
expediently.

Displays flexibility, team spirit, compassion, respect, politeness and accountability when dealing with customers. Demonstrates awareness of and sensitivity for patient’s rights in all interfaces with
patients.

 AREAS OF EMPHASIS

·        Instructs and prepares patients for examinations and assists Radiologist during procedures.

·        Demonstrates competence and proficiency in operation of all equipment and reports malfunctions to manager or lead.

·        Completes all assigned procedures in a timely manner and uses time between procedures to department advantage-assisting others and or assisting with room turnover

·        Employee understands his/her role on maintaining medication security to authorized personnel only.

·        Demonstrates competency in providing health care services in own job category to all age groups.

·        Demonstrates competency in interpersonal relationships to all age groups.

DOCUMENTATION/INFORMATION MANAGEMENT

·        Demonstrates competency in the use of Hospital information System (HIS), Radiology Information System (RIS) and PACS.

·        Participates in hospital performance improvement activities (BPI projects, task forces,
etc.) when warranted.

·        Demonstrate confidentiality using own password and all patient care modules.

·        Answers telephone inquiries of a technical nature from physicians or hospital personnel
in other areas of the hospital while maintaining good phone etiquette.

Requirements:

·        BLS (American Heart Association ONLY)

·        Two (2) years’ experience

·        NYS License (or able to obtain prior to start)

Preferred:

·        VIR Certified-preferred
